content: State-of-the-art 2025 active learning memory agents can leverage a biologically inspired lateral inhibition gating mechanism to dynamically suppress weakly relevant retrieved memory traces, amplifying the signal of high-value, information-dense data for efficient active query selection. This solves a key open challenge in modern memory-augmented agents: reducing active learning annotation waste caused by noisy, low-value redundant memory retrievals.
source_topics: active learning memory agent SOTA deep 2025
